topic.name: Renewable Fuel Standards
topic.description: Legislation aimed at promoting the use of renewable fuels in transportation to reduce reliance on fossil fuels.

Document 1: Open Fuel Standard Act of 2013 - Requires each fleet of a manufacturer of passenger automobiles to comprise at least: (1) 30% qualified vehicles in model year 2016, and (2) 50% qualified vehicles in model year 2017
Defines 'qualified vehicle' as: (1) a vehicle that operates on natural gas, hydrogen, or biodiesel
Document 2: This bill amends the Clean Air Act by revising the renewable fuel program, which requires transportation fuel to contain a minimum volume of renewable fuel
The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) must cap the total volume of ethanol blended into the transportation fuel supply at 9.7% of projected gasoline demand
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to redefine the term 'cellulosic biomass ethanol' to mean ethanol derived from any cellulose, hemicellulose, or lignin that is derived from renewable biomass
Increases the volume of renewable fuel that gasoline is required to contain for 2008-2012.

topic.name: Water Conservation Projects
topic.description: Legislation focused on the design, planning, and construction of water recycling and conservation projects across various regions in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Lower Rio Grande Valley Water Resources Conservation and Improvement Act of 2000 to authorize specified additional projects, including projects for water conservation and improvement.
Permits each project that the Secretary of the Interior... to include: the replacement of irrigation canals and lateral canals with buried pipelines.
Document 2: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of: recycled water distribution systems in California.
Limits the federal share of each project to 25%.
Document 3: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Department of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water system facilities in California.
The federal share of each project is limited to 25%.

topic.name: Medicare and Prescription Drug Access
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving Medicare beneficiary access to healthcare services and prescription drugs through contractual agreements and cost-sharing regulations.

Document 3: prohibit a health plan offering group or individual health insurance that provides coverage for prescription drugs
imposing co-payment, coinsurance, or other cost-sharing requirements applicable to prescription drugs in a specialty drug tier

topic.name: Employment Discrimination and Labor Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at protecting workers from discrimination based on age and enhancing their rights to organize and engage in collective bargaining.

Document 1: Amends the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967 to specify that an unlawful employment practice is established when the complaining party demonstrates that age or participation in investigations...
Prohibits the court in such an instance from awarding damages or issuing an order requiring any admission, reinstatement, hiring, promotion, or payment.
Document 2: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to certify a bargaining representative without directing an election if a majority of the bargaining unit employees have authorized designation of the representative...
Adds to remedies for such violations: (1) back pay plus liquidated damages; and (2) additional civil penalties.
Document 3: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board to certify a bargaining representative without directing an election if a majority of the bargaining unit employees have authorized designation of the representative...
Adds to remedies for such violations: (1) back pay plus liquidated damages; and (2) additional civil penalties.

topic.name: Employment and Child Welfare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster addresses legislative amendments related to employment services and child welfare programs in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Wagner-Peyser Act to include the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands in the U.S. Employment Service
providing public employment services through a one-stop services delivery system.
Document 2: Amends part E (Foster Care and Adoption Assistance) of title IV of the Social Security Act
eliminate the requirement that, to be eligible for foster care maintenance payments, a child would have been eligible for aid under the former Aid to Families with Dependent Children (AFDC) program.
Document 3: Amends part E (Foster Care and Adoption Assistance) of title IV of the Social Security Act
expresses the sense of the Congress that: (1) the AFDC eligibility requirement for federal foster care and adoption assistance benefits should be eliminated.

topic.name: Regulatory Accountability and State Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing regulatory accountability while safeguarding state rights, particularly in relation to federal rulemaking.

Document 1: Restoring the 10th Amendment Act - Authorizes a designated state official to file with the head of a federal agency proposing a rule, during the period when the proposed rule is required to be open for public comment, a legal brief challenging the constitutionality of the rule under the Tenth Amendment.
Directs the relevant U.S. Court of Appeals, at the request of a designated state official, to grant expedited review of a decision by a district court in such a case.
Document 2: Unfunded Mandates Accountability Act of 2011 - Amends the Unfunded Mandates Reform Act of 1995 to require regulatory impact analyses for rules that do not involve a legislative mandate.

Require federal agencies to prepare and publish in the Federal Register an initial and final regulatory impact analysis prior to promulgating any proposed or final rule that may have an annual effect on the economy of $100 million or more.

topic.name: Data Privacy and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at protecting personal data and regulating information services, including accountability measures for data brokers and surveillance practices.

Document 3: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 to require the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) to include a statement that the information sought is relevant to an authorized investigation
Requires such statement to enumerate the minimization (protection) procedures adopted by the Attorney General applicable to FBI retention and dissemination of any information made available.

topic.name: Real Estate Financial Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments related to real estate transactions, focusing on consumer protections and the regulation of financial institutions in real estate activities.

Document 1: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to prohibit financial holding companies and national banks from engaging in real estate brokerage or management activities.
Exempts certain activities related to managing real property owned by national banks.
Document 2: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to requirements for disclosure to a consumer of points and fees information about a consumer credit transaction.
Modifies the criteria for exclusion from the computation of points and fees of certain reasonable charges.
Document 3: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to requirements for disclosure to a consumer of points and fees information.
Specifies all compensation paid directly by a consumer to a mortgage originator.

topic.name: Transportation and Urban Development
topic.description: This topic encompasses strategies and initiatives aimed at enhancing transportation infrastructure and promoting sustainable urban development, particularly in nonmetropolitan areas and developing countries.

Document 1: Requires states to cooperate with affected nonmetropolitan local officials responsible for transportation
emphasis on addressing the transportation needs of nonmetropolitan areas
Document 2: Directs the Administrator of the United States Agency for International Development (USAID) to develop a strategy to foster sustainable urban development
analyze approaches to improve environmental sustainability in urban areas
Document 3: Establishes in the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) the United States Global Development Lab
leveraging the expertise and resources of businesses, nongovernmental organizations, science and research organizations, and universities to increase program impact

topic.name: Agricultural Policy Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments related to agricultural practices, specifically addressing the management of crops and wildlife in the context of natural disasters and farming flexibility.

Document 1: Amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act to allow an agricultural producer to take migratory game birds by the aid of baiting
if the manipulation occurs on the producer's land on which a crop was not harvestable due to a natural disaster
Document 2: Amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act to allow an agricultural producer to take migratory game birds by the aid of baiting
if the manipulation occurs on the producer's land on which a crop was not harvestable due to a natural disaster
Document 3: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 to consider mung beans and pulse crops as covered commodities
as of crop year 2014 producers on a farm may reduce the base acres for any covered commodity for a crop year by one acre for each acre used for the production of fruits or vegetables

topic.name: Veterans Benefits and Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing financial support and benefits for veterans and their families, particularly regarding funeral expenses, survivor benefits, and caregiving leave.

Document 1: Increases the authorized allowance for burial and funeral expenses for deceased veterans
increases the burial plot allowance for veterans who were receiving hospital or nursing home care
Document 2: Repeals certain provisions which require the offset of amounts paid in dependency and indemnity compensation from Survivor Benefit Plan annuities
Directs the Secretary concerned to restore annuity eligibility to a surviving spouse
Document 3: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 to entitle an eligible employee to a total of 26 workweeks of leave
Defines covered servicemember as a member of the U.S. Armed Forces undergoing medical treatment

topic.name: Pulmonary Hypertension Awareness and Research
topic.description: Legislative efforts focused on enhancing research, education, and awareness regarding pulmonary hypertension and its treatment.

Document 1: the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) should continue aggressive work on pulmonary hypertension
Requires the Secretary to carry out an educational campaign to increase public awareness of pulmonary hypertension
Document 2: the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) should continue aggressive work on pulmonary hypertension
Requires the Secretary to carry out an educational campaign to increase awareness of pulmonary hypertension among health care providers

topic.name: Middle School Improvement Grants
topic.description: The documents discuss federal initiatives to provide grants aimed at improving middle school education, particularly for schools serving high proportions of disadvantaged students.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17
implement state middle grades needs analyses and improvement plans
Document 2: directs the Department of Education (ED) to allot grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17
award competitive subgrants to local educational agencies (LEAs) or partnerships of LEAs
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17
Gives priority to LEAs that serve high proportions of poor children

topic.name: Land Management and Tribal Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to land management, conservation, and the rights of Native American tribes in the United States.

Document 1: Requires all interest of the United States in approximately 14,408 acres of land to be held in trust for the Confederated Tribes of Coos, Lower Umpqua, and Siuslaw Indians.
Prohibits gaming on such land.
Document 2: Designates certain areas in California as wilderness areas and components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to Elko County, Nevada, approximately 275 acres of land managed by the Bureau of Land Management.
Holds in trust lands for various Indian tribes, making them part of their respective Reservations.

topic.name: Veteran Small Business Programs
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing opportunities for service-disabled veteran-owned small businesses in federal contracting and development grants.

Document 1: Disabled Veteran Small Business Eligibility Expansion Act of 2007
make small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans eligible for the award by the Small Business Administration (SBA)
Document 2: Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business Equity Act of 2007
authorize the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to award contracts to small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans
Document 3: Small Business Contracting Programs Parity Act of 2009
authorize a contracting opportunity to be awarded under the program on the basis of competition restricted to qualified HUBZone small businesses

topic.name: Reuniting Families Act
topic.description: Legislation aimed at amending immigration laws to increase family-sponsored and employment-based immigration visas in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish the fiscal year worldwide level of employment-based immigrants at 140,000 plus: (1) the previous year's unused visas; and (2) the number of unused visas from FY1992-FY2007.
Establishes the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants at 480,000 plus: (1) the previous year's unused visas; and (2) the number of unused visas from FY1992-FY2007.
Document 2: Revises the definition of 'immediate relative' to: (1) mean a child, spouse, or parent of a U.S. citizen or lawful permanent resident.
Increases immigration visas for: (1) unmarried sons and daughters of U.S. citizens; and (2) brothers and sisters of U.S. citizens.
Document 3: Provides a 60,000 visa allocation for the unmarried ...
Permit a widow or widower of a U.S. citizen or resident to seek permanent resident status if married at least two years at the time of the citizen's or resident's death.

topic.name: Gun Control Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the regulation and rights surrounding firearm possession and carrying, particularly concerning concealed carry and domestic violence protections.

Document 1: Respecting States Rights and Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act of 2008
reciprocity for the carrying of certain concealed firearms in different states
Document 2: Respecting States Rights and Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act of 2009
reciprocity for the carrying of certain concealed firearms in states that allow their residents to carry such firearms
Document 3: Protecting Domestic Violence and Stalking Victims Act of 2015
makes it a crime to knowingly sell or dispose of a firearm to a person who has been convicted of a misdemeanor crime of stalking

topic.name: Federal Transportation Funding
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the allocation and management of federal funds for transportation infrastructure, including highways, harbors, and public transit systems.

Document 1: Requires the total budget resources for expenditures from the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund for harbor maintenance programs to equal the level of receipts plus interest credited to such Fund for that fiscal year.
Limits the use of such resources to such programs only.
Document 2: Revises requirements for the equity bonus program for specified federal-aid highway programs for FY2010-FY2015.
Increases from 92% to 105% the percentage for such a state of the total fiscal year apportionments for the programs.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Transportation to make emergency grants to states and designated recipients for the operating costs of equipment and facilities for use in public transportation projects.
Sets the amount of a grant, at the option of the recipient, at up to 100% of the net cost of a project.

topic.name: Federal Program Evaluation and Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses the evaluation, reporting, and reform of federal programs and services to enhance efficiency and effectiveness within government operations.

Document 1: requires the annual reports of federal agency chief financial officers to include the cost of multilingual services
issue guidance for agency chief financial officers in compiling reports on the cost of multilingual services
Document 2: establishes the Federal Program Sunset Commission to evaluate the efficiency and public need for each federal program
submit to Congress and the President an annual report containing a list of federal programs that the Commission recommends to be abolished or reorganized
Document 3: establishes the Commission on Government Transformation
assess federal programs for economy, efficiency, and effectiveness and identify best practices

topic.name: Military Deployment and Readiness
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the deployment of U.S. military forces, their readiness, and the management of military operations in conflict zones.

Document 1: Amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 1993
expand the indemnification responsibilities of the Department of Defense
Document 2: Directs the President to transition the mission of U.S. forces in Iraq
Prohibits funds from being obligated or expended to continue the U.S. Armed Forces deployment in Iraq
Document 3: Prohibits any unit of the regular Armed Forces from being deployed for Operations Iraqi Freedom
optimal minimum period between such deployments should be equal to or longer than twice the period of the most recent previous deployment

topic.name: U.S. Trade Regulations
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the suspension of duties on specific acrylic materials and the implications of sanctions related to Syria.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic filament tow
with an average decitex of 4.1
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic fiber tow
with an average decitex of 3.3
Document 3: Maintains U.S. restrictions against Syria
Imposes specified trade, assistance, admissions, and military sanctions

topic.name: Pharmaceutical Drug Importation Regulations
topic.description: This topic covers the legislative measures and regulations regarding the importation of prescription drugs, focusing on safety, compliance, and consumer education.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to revise provisions governing the importation of prescription drugs.
Prohibits the importation of a qualifying drug unless such drug is imported by: (1) a registered importer; or (2) an individual for personal use.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to revise provisions governing the importation of prescription drugs.
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to inspect places of business, verify chains of custody, inspect facilities, and determine compliance with registration conditions.
Document 3: Prohibits manufacturers from: (1) discriminating against registered exporters or importers; (2) causing there to be a difference in a prescription drug distributed in the United States and one distributed in a permitted country.
Requires the Secretary to educate consumers regarding prescription drug importation.

topic.name: Natural Gas and Oil Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulatory measures governing the exploration, production, and trade of natural gas and oil resources in the United States, particularly in relation to the Outer Continental Shelf and federal lands.

Document 1: prohibit oil and gas preleasing, leasing, and related activities in areas of the Outer Continental Shelf located off the coast of California
exclude such areas from the waters of the U.S. Outer Continental Shelf
Document 2: authorize the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating to issue a certificate of documentation with a coastwise endorsement for the vessels LNG GEMINI, LNG LEO, and LNG VIRGO
Limits authorized coastwise trade for each vessel to the carriage of natural gas
Document 3: authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to accept bids on any new oil and gas leases of federal lands

prohibit granting a new right-of-way for a natural gas pipeline unless the right-of-way applicant certifies that all natural gas transported via such pipeline will be offered for sale only in the United States

topic.name: Campaign Finance and Transparency
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing transparency and accountability in campaign finance and education loan obligations.

Document 1: This bill amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more to the Federal Election Commission (FEC)

This requirement, with certain additions, shall now apply to any political committee, not just a candidate's principal campaign committee, regarding cumulative contributions of $1,000 or more from any contributor during a calendar year.
Document 2: This bill amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more to the Federal Election Commission (FEC)

This requirement, with certain additions, shall now apply to any political committee, not just a candidate's principal campaign committee, regarding cumulative contributions of $1,000 or more from any contributor during a calendar year.
Document 3: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require lenders of private education loans to describe clearly and conspicuously, in writing, the cosigners' obligations regarding such loans
Directs the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) to publish a model form for describing a cosigner's obligations regarding private education loans.

topic.name: Tax Reform for Businesses
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at reforming tax policies for businesses, particularly small businesses and S corporations.

Document 1: amend the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the tax credit for increasing research activities
allow a qualified small business to use a portion of its tax credit for increasing research expenditures as an offset against its employment tax liability.
Document 2: amends the Internal Revenue Code to revise the tax treatment of S corporations
allowing S corporations to increase passive investment income from 25 to 60% without incurring additional tax.
Document 3: amends the Internal Revenue Code to reduce to 23% the maximum income tax rate for corporations
make permanent the increased ($250,000) expensing allowance for depreciable business assets.
